name: Zinc Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Zinc. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Zinc oxide removal without substrate damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength with good Zinc absorption and minimal reflectivity

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for effective Zinc oxide removal

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for continuous cleaning with thermal management

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for Zinc oxide ablation without melting substrate

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for efficient Zinc ablation with thermal control

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform Zinc surface cleaning

passCount.description: Number of passes for thorough Zinc oxide removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for complete Zinc surface coverage

power.description: Typical range for industrial 1064 nm fiber lasers cleaning reflective metals like zinc to avoid excessive heat buildup; starting at 150 W balances efficiency and safety. Max limited to prevent melting (zinc MP ~419°C). Source: CleanLaser and IPG Photonics equipment specs for metal surface preparation.

scan_speed.description: Range ensures effective contaminant removal without substrate ablation; slower for heavy contamination (e.g., oils), faster for light rust. Recommended 1500 mm/s for optimal zinc cleaning to minimize thermal effects due to high reflectivity. Source: Industry standards from Laserax and academic reviews in J. Laser Appl. (2018).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective industrial cleaning of zinc; shorter pulses (ps/fs) reduce HAZ but are less common at 1064 nm. 50 ns recommended for minimal heat diffusion in zinc's high thermal conductivity (~116 W/mK). Source: Laser physics studies in Opt. Express (2015) on metal ablation thresholds.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed fiber lasers; higher rates speed up cleaning but increase heat accumulation on reflective zinc, risking damage. 50 kHz balances speed and safety. Source: Academic literature in Appl. Surf. Sci. (2020) on pulse train effects for metal cleaning.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for contaminant ablation without zinc damage; threshold ~2.5 J/cm² due to high reflectivity at 1064 nm. Recommended 1.5 J/cm² for safe removal of oxides/contaminants. Conservative to account for zinc's low melting point. Source: Ablation threshold studies in J. Phys. D: Appl. Phys. (2017).

spot_size.description: Smaller spots for precision on zinc details, larger for broad areas; 200 μm recommended for uniform energy distribution and reduced peak fluence on reflective surfaces. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent and Trumpf laser cleaning systems.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy layers; 2 passes recommended for zinc to ensure complete removal without cumulative heating. Source: Industry case studies in Surf. Coat. Technol. (2019) on multi-pass laser cleaning of metals.

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage without gaps or overexposure; 60% optimal for zinc to prevent uneven heating on large areas. Source: Process optimization in Laser Technol. J. (2021), typical for industrial scanning parameters.
